# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import { get } from '@vercel/edge-config';

interface EdgeConfigData {
  API_KEY?: string;
  DB_URL?: string;
  FEATURE_FLAGS?: {
    enableNewUI?: boolean;
    enableBetaFeatures?: boolean;
    maxRequestsPerMinute?: number;
  };
}

const DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
  API_KEY: 'vercel_default_key_789',
  DB_URL: 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com',
  FEATURE_FLAGS: {
    enableNewUI: false,
    enableBetaFeatures: false,
    maxRequestsPerMinute: 100,
  },
};

function sanitizeUrl(url: string): string {
  try {
    const parsed = new URL(url);
    const allowedProtocols = ['postgresql:', 'postgres:', 'mysql:', 'https:', 'http:'];
    
    if (!allowedProtocols.includes(parsed.protocol)) {
      throw new Error('Invalid protocol');
    }
    
    return url;
  } catch {
    throw new Error('Invalid URL format');
  }
}

function validateApiKey(key: string): boolean {
  if (typeof key !== 'string' || key.length < 10 || key.length > 256) {
    return false;
  }
  
  const validPattern = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]+$/;
  return validPattern.test(key);
}

function sanitizeFeatureFlags(flags: any): EdgeConfigData['FEATURE_FLAGS'] {
  const sanitized: EdgeConfigData['FEATURE_FLAGS'] = {
    enableNewUI: false,
    enableBetaFeatures: false,
    maxRequestsPerMinute: 100,
  };

  if (typeof flags === 'object' && flags !== null) {
    if (typeof flags.enableNewUI === 'boolean') {
      sanitized.enableNewUI = flags.enableNewUI;
    }
    if (typeof flags.enableBetaFeatures === 'boolean') {
      sanitized.enableBetaFeatures = flags.enableBetaFeatures;
    }
    if (typeof flags.maxRequestsPerMinute === 'number' && 
        flags.maxRequestsPerMinute > 0 && 
        flags.maxRequestsPerMinute <= 10000) {
      sanitized.maxRequestsPerMinute = flags.maxRequestsPerMinute;
    }
  }

  return sanitized;
}

async function getSecureConfig(): Promise<EdgeConfigData> {
  const config: EdgeConfigData = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG };

  if (!process.env.EDGE_CONFIG) {
    return config;
  }

  try {
    const edgeConfigData = await get<EdgeConfigData>('config');

    if (edgeConfigData) {
      if (edgeConfigData.API_KEY && validateApiKey(edgeConfigData.API_KEY)) {
        config.API_KEY = edgeConfigData.API_KEY;
      }

      if (edgeConfigData.DB_URL) {
        try {
          config.DB_URL = sanitizeUrl(edgeConfigData.DB_URL);
        } catch {
          config.DB_URL = DEFAULT_CONFIG.DB_URL;
        }
      }

      if (edgeConfigData.FEATURE_FLAGS) {
        config.FEATURE_FLAGS = sanitizeFeatureFlags(edgeConfigData.FEATURE_FLAGS);
      }
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Failed to fetch Edge Config, using defaults');
  }

  return config;
}

function maskSensitiveData(data: string): string {
  if (data.length <= 8) {
    return '***';
  }
  return data.substring(0, 4) + '***' + data.substring(data.length - 4);
}

function createSafeConfigResponse(config: EdgeConfigData) {
  const safeDbUrl = config.DB_URL ? config.DB_URL.replace(/:\/\/([^:]+):([^@]+)@/, '://$1:***@') : '';
  
  return {
    apiKey: config.API_KEY ? maskSensitiveData(config.API_KEY) : '',
    dbUrl: safeDbUrl,
    featureFlags: config.FEATURE_FLAGS,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  };
}

export default async function handler(req: NextRequest) {
  const allowedOrigins = [
    'https://vercel.com',
    'https://vercel.app',
  ];

  const origin = req.headers.get('origin') || '';
  const isAllowedOrigin = allowedOrigins.some(allowed => origin.startsWith(allowed));

  if (req.method !== 'GET' && req.method !== 'POST') {
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: 'Method not allowed' },
      { status: 405 }
    );
  }

  try {
    const config = await getSecureConfig();
    
    const safeResponse = createSafeConfigResponse(config);

    const headers: Record<string, string> = {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff',
      'X-Frame-Options': 'DENY',
      'X-XSS-Protection': '1; mode=block',
      'Strict-Transport-Security': 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains',
      'Cache-Control': 'private, no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate',
    };

    if (isAllowedOrigin) {
      headers['Access-Control-Allow-Origin'] = origin;
      headers['Access-Control-Allow-Credentials'] = 'true';
    }

    return NextResponse.json(
      {
        success: true,
        config: safeResponse,
        source: process.env.EDGE_CONFIG ? 'edge-config' : 'defaults',
      },
      { 
        status: 200,
        headers,
      }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.json(
      { 
        success: false, 
        error: 'Internal server error',
      },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};
